chunk_summary: The National Vitamin A Supplementation (VAS) schedule in India provides age-based prophylaxis for children aged 9 months to 5 years, emphasizing proper documentation, spacing, and safe administration to prevent double dosing or missed protection.
learning_objectives: Understand the age-based National VAS prophylaxis schedule for children in India.
Learn the importance of proper documentation and spacing of VAS doses.
Identify safe administration practices and manage potential adverse effects of high-dose Vitamin A.
Recognize the role of community platforms in delivering VAS.
key_facts: The first VAS dose (1 mL, 1 lakh IU) is given at 9 months, alongside the MR vaccine.
The second dose (2 mL, 2 lakh IU) is given at 16–18 months, followed by doses 3–9 every 6 months until 5 years of age.
VAS is administered orally; avoid dosing if the child is vomiting or unable to swallow safely.
Proper documentation is critical: verify the last dose date and schedule the next dose at least 6 months later.
Transient nausea or vomiting may occur after high-dose Vitamin A but is usually self-limited.
Repeated closely spaced dosing increases the risk of adverse effects without added benefit.
reference_quote: The first dose is given at 9 months (1 mL containing 1 lakh IU) typically alongside measles-rubella (MR) vaccination, followed by a second dose at 16–18 months (2 mL containing 2 lakh IU).
document_key_topics: National VAS prophylaxis schedule for children
Documentation and spacing of Vitamin A doses
Safe administration practices for high-dose Vitamin A
